en.value:
Prevention services through establishing linkages with the existing government services in the source, transit and destination locations.
	
		Treatment, care and support through establishing linkages with public and private service providers at source and destination points to ensure continuity of services across the continuum of mobility without interruption
	
		Reducing the vulnerability of mobile women in sex work and wives of mobile men at the source areas
	
		Establishing and strengthening community based organizations, local government and NGO’s to reduce vulnerability of mobile populations
	
		Linking NGOs and CBOs at source, transit and destination through use of appropriate information and communication technology and establishment of a database to connect between mobile populations and their families in source, transit and destination areas
	
		Sensitizing and orienting people with whom mobile populations will come into contact such as transport organizations, highway hoteliers, traders, border security personnel,  customs and other local officials at transit points; improving their ability and effectiveness in advising mobile populations, with a particular focus on the specific vulnerabilities and denial of rights faced by mobile women.
	
		A regional research centre - the project envisages the establishment of a regional research centre on HIV and mobility, managed by The Overseas Development Institute (ODI), which will house all the information and data about mobility and HIV in the region and undertake specific research, surveys and studies on a regular basis to ensure availability and quality of information to support the efforts of evidence based advocacy at national and regional levels
	
		Research studies - will be conducted to better understand the gender dynamics and gender based vulnerabilities of mobile populations and their family members
	
		Monitoring and Evaluation - The project management unit will work with the research centre to monitor and evaluate the project, in order to guarantee that the learning cycle is active and constantly feeds into more effective implementation, resulting in better practice

en.full: Enhancing Mobile Population Access to HIV/AIDs Service, Information & Support (EMPHASIS)

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
